project_structure: pnpm monorepo with three packages: packages/shared (zod schemas + types + i18n locales), packages/server (MCP HTTP service + serve-lock state machine), packages/cli (citty command surface: install/scan/serve/uninstall/doctor/hooks/plan-context-hint). Schema source of truth is packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts; runtime audit-mode consumers are ZERO (audit-mode is declared in schema and typed in packages/shared/src/types/config.ts but no server/cli code path reads it — only test fixtures exercise it). CLI test architecture is anchored by a CLI surface drift gate (packages/cli/__tests__/cli-surface.test.ts) that snapshots every flag of every command into __snapshots__/cli-surface.test.ts.snap, so ANY flag rename/kill from rc.15 mandates simultaneous snapshot update + critical-flag assertion update + docs/test-seed/cli.md §1 sync.

relevant_files.rationale: Direct C10 modify_target: lines 52-53 declare BOTH `auditMode` (camelCase, line 52) and `audit_mode` (snake_case, line 53) as optional auditModeSchema fields. The duplicate key is a historical bug from a half-finished rename. rc.15 must delete line 52 and keep line 53 (snake_case matches sibling keys like `archive_hint_hours`, `review_hint_pending_count`, `import_window_first_run_months`).

key_code.description: DELETE in rc.15 C10. `auditMode: auditModeSchema.optional()` — the camelCase legacy duplicate. No runtime consumer reads this field; only test fixtures.
KEEP in rc.15 C10. `audit_mode: auditModeSchema.optional()` — snake_case, aligned with archive_hint_hours / review_hint_pending_count / fabric_language naming convention. Becomes the sole spelling.
`z.enum(['strict', 'warn', 'off'])` — the enum itself is unchanged; only the field-name dedupe is in scope.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Schema-and-test-impact angle: this file is the single point of definition for the C10 dedupe. Every test file referencing `auditMode` traces back here.

relevant_files.rationale: Mirror of the zod schema for the TypeScript-facing FabricConfig interface. Lines 32-33 carry the SAME duplicate `auditMode?: AuditMode` (L32) and `audit_mode?: AuditMode` (L33). Must delete L32 in lockstep with the schema change or the TS type drifts from the zod-inferred type.

key_code.description: DELETE in rc.15 C10. `auditMode?: AuditMode;` — TS mirror of the schema duplicate.
KEEP in rc.15 C10. `audit_mode?: AuditMode;` — TS mirror of the canonical key.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Schema-and-test-impact angle: the zod schema and TS type must stay in sync; this is the second of two files defining the field shape.

relevant_files.rationale: Test fixture at L108 uses `auditMode: 'strict' as const` inside the `previousVersionFixture` regression test, then asserts `expect(parsed.auditMode).toBe('strict')` at L117. Both lines break post-dedupe: parse will SILENTLY succeed (zod ignores unknown extras because fabricConfigSchema is NOT .strict()) but `parsed.auditMode` becomes undefined and the assertion fails.

key_code.description: Legacy v2.0-rc.1-shaped config fixture; L108 carries `auditMode: 'strict' as const`. Update to `audit_mode: 'strict' as const`.
Assertion on the parsed result; must change to `parsed.audit_mode`.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Schema-and-test-impact angle: this is the canonical regression test for fabric-config schema parsing — the place where the dedupe most visibly breaks.

relevant_files.rationale: L52 inside the I1.1 'full config with all fields' round-trip test passes `auditMode: 'strict'` to roundTrip(fabricConfigSchema, ...). Since the schema is not .strict(), the round-trip still succeeds but the parsed output drops the unknown `auditMode` — the round-trip assertion (input deep-equals output) will fail.

key_code.symbol: I1.1 fabricConfigSchema round-trip — full config with all fields
key_code.description: Replace `auditMode: 'strict'` with `audit_mode: 'strict'` on L52.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Schema-and-test-impact angle: round-trip property defends the invariant that schema.parse is identity on valid inputs — the dedupe's only test-side break here is the field-name swap.

relevant_files.rationale: fast-check arbitrary at L44-L62 generates BOTH `auditMode` (L49) AND `audit_mode` (L54) as optional record keys. Post-dedupe, the L49 arbitrary must be deleted entirely; only L54 (snake_case) remains.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Schema-and-test-impact angle: property-based tests generate config shapes randomly — leaving the camelCase arbitrary would generate invalid configs in the post-dedupe world.

relevant_files.rationale: THE rc.15 test-impact epicenter. L83-L88 snapshots EVERY flag of install/scan/doctor/serve/uninstall via toMatchSnapshot. L108-L113 hard-asserts `install exposes critical flags --force / --scope / --reapply`. L94-L103 hard-asserts the public command set is `{install, scan, doctor, serve, uninstall}` (no `hooks` — already off-list). EVERY rc.15 flag kill / rename touches this file.

key_code.description: Top-level drift gate. After rc.15 must (a) update L108 critical-flag assertion if --force is killed across install/uninstall/serve/doctor or if --reapply is killed, (b) optionally re-include `scan` in the public command set assertion if scan command is deleted, (c) leave `hooks` out (already not exported as public; verify hooks command kill removes from packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts).
Currently expects `[doctor, install, scan, serve, uninstall]`. If C8 deletes the scan command, this must shrink to `[doctor, install, serve, uninstall]`.

Currently expects install to have `force, scope, reapply`. If --force / --reapply are killed in rc.15, this assertion must be relaxed (e.g. only `scope`) or rewritten to assert `dry-run` (the renamed --plan).

relevant_files.topic_relation: Schema-and-test-impact angle: this is the single drift-gate that catches every CLI surface mutation. Cannot land rc.15 without updating this file.

Every rc.15 flag kill/rename requires regenerating this snapshot (vitest -u).

relevant_files.rationale: Hosts --force on serve. L46 args declaration; L61 `acquireLock(projectRoot, {force: args.force})`. Killing serve's --force means a serve attempting to start while another live PID holds the lock will ALWAYS throw — the rationale from the task notes is the user gets ServeLockHeldError.actionHint which currently says 'Stop the other process (PID X) or run with --force to override' — that hint must be rewritten too.

relevant_files.rationale: Per cli-surface.test.ts DRIFT_HINT (L20-L24), this seed file documents the §1 Feature Surface. Every rc.15 flag kill must update §1 BEFORE running `vitest -u`. Counter-process: snapshot regen WITHOUT seed update is the leading cause of stale drift gates.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Schema-and-test-impact angle: the human-readable surface contract that the drift gate enforces. Authoritative for what the post-rc.15 surface SHOULD look like.

patterns: Two patterns dominate the rc.15 cleanup surface. PATTERN 1 (audit_mode rename, scoped, mechanical): only 5 files touch auditMode — schema (fabric-config.ts L52), type (config.ts L32), and three test files (fabric-config.test.ts L108/L117, schemas-roundtrip.test.ts L52, zod-roundtrip.test.ts L49-L53). Zero runtime consumers in cli/ or server/. Apply rg-driven find-and-replace: kill `auditMode` from schema+type; in tests change `auditMode: 'strict'` → `audit_mode: 'strict'` and `parsed.auditMode` → `parsed.audit_mode`. Clean-slate per `feedback_clean_slate.md` memory — no zod preprocess shim. PATTERN 2 (CLI surface drift gate cascade): every flag kill/rename triggers a 4-step ripple: (a) update citty arg declaration in packages/cli/src/commands/<cmd>.ts, (b) update i18n keys in packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/{en,zh-CN}.ts, (c) update test args & assertions in packages/cli/__tests__/<test>.ts + integration/<test>.ts, (d) regenerate __snapshots__/cli-surface.test.ts.snap and __snapshots__/i18n.test.ts.snap via `vitest -u`, (e) update docs/test-seed/cli.md §1 to match new surface. Skipping any step breaks the drift gate. Test-impact pattern: 4 SNAPSHOT files (cli-surface.snap, i18n.snap, mcp-config-merge.snap, client-configs.snap) — only the first two are flag-sensitive. Hard-coded substring assertions are the highest-risk silent-breakers: cli-surface.test.ts L110-L125 critical-flag assertArrayContaining; install-cli-surface.test.ts L217 'Using standard --plan mode' string; install-diff-mode.test.ts L254 `/--force.*legacy escape hatch|逃生口/` regex; doctor.test.ts L287 `.includes('apply-lint')`.
dependencies: INTERNAL: packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts:178 exports runInitScan() consumed by packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts:926 (after init writes scaffold) AND by 12 test cases in scan-init.test.ts. Critical preservation constraint: scan COMMAND deletion (C8) must NOT delete the runInitScan function — only the citty defineCommand wrapper. packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts:107-108 calls checkLockOrThrow from packages/server/src/services/serve-lock.ts:92 with `{force: args.force}` — the force arg propagates from CLI to server. Same pattern install.ts:373 + uninstall.ts:259 + serve.ts:61. AcquireOptions.force in serve-lock.ts can be fully deleted post-rc.15 since no caller passes it. packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts exports installHooks() consumed by 2 test files; deletion (C8) must preserve installHooks as a non-command export (perhaps moved into packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts where its imports already originate). EXTERNAL: zod (schema parsing — non-strict default means dropping `auditMode` from schema silently DROPS the field at parse time rather than erroring; tests must explicitly assert on parsed shape, not on parse-throws). citty (CLI framework — flag rename = arg-record key change; killing a flag means removing the key from the args object in defineCommand). vitest (toMatchSnapshot + vi.hoisted for FAB_LANG=en pinning).
integration_points: Lock-conflict UX surface (HIGH RISK): packages/server/src/services/serve-lock.ts:52 + L104 — both ServeLockHeldError construction sites embed actionHint strings that reference '--force to override'. Post-rc.15 these MUST be rewritten or the runtime error message dangles users with non-existent CLI flag advice. CLI surface drift gate: packages/cli/__tests__/cli-surface.test.ts is the single canonical contract — adding/removing a flag flows through citty arg declaration → cli-surface.test.ts.snap snapshot → docs/test-seed/cli.md §1 manual sync (per DRIFT_HINT L18-L24 of cli-surface.test.ts). i18n key propagation: every flag.description string lives in packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts + zh-CN.ts, threaded into help text and into stdout/stderr capture for the i18n.test.ts.snap — i18n locale updates ripple into 2 snapshot files automatically. doctor --rescan integration: the new flag would call runInitScan(target, {source: 'doctor'}) at packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts:107 (post-lock-check, pre-runDoctor) — runInitScan is already exported from scan.ts:178 and accepts arbitrary `source` strings. No source-tracking schema change needed.
constraints: ZERO-USER CLEAN-SLATE: per memory `feedback_clean_slate.md` Fabric has no published users (v2.0 RC chain never tagged on npm). C10 must NOT add a zod preprocess alias or a 'legacy auditMode detected' warning. Direct removal only. v2.0.0-rc.15 IS the publish target — the rc.14 TASK-002 deprecation banner (en.ts L216, zh-CN.ts L211) literally promises '--force will be removed in v2.0.0-rc.15' — this rc must deliver. NON-STRICT SCHEMA: fabricConfigSchema is z.object({...}) NOT .strict() (only clientPathsSchema is .strict() per L9-L16 of fabric-config.ts). Consequence: after C10, a config carrying `auditMode: 'strict'` will parse SUCCESSFULLY but silently DROP the field — leading to surprising no-op behavior. If we wanted hard-fail-on-unknown-key we'd need to add .strict() to fabricConfigSchema, but that's a separate, riskier change. Recommend: leave non-strict to preserve forward-compat for future config additions; tests must explicitly assert the parsed shape, never assume parse-throws. runInitScan PRESERVATION: deleting the scan command must not break install.ts:926 or the 12 test cases in scan-init.test.ts that drive runInitScan directly. Best practice: keep scan.ts file alive, only delete the citty defineCommand wrapper and remove from commands/index.ts. installHooks PRESERVATION: same pattern — hook-config validation logic in hooks.ts L36+ is consumed by 2 test files via the installHooks named export. CLI surface tests at packages/cli/__tests__/cli-surface.test.ts run BEFORE snapshot — adding `--rescan` to doctor requires citty arg declaration first, otherwise the snapshot won't include it.

clarification_needs.question: ServeLockHeldError actionHint post-`--force` removal: how should the runtime message change?
clarification_needs.context: packages/server/src/services/serve-lock.ts:52 and L104 both construct ServeLockHeldError with actionHint strings that reference '--force to override' — `'Stop the other process (PID ${state.pid}) or run with --force to override'` (L52) and `'Stop the other serve process (PID ${state.pid}) before running this command, or pass --force to override'` (L104). After rc.15 kills --force from doctor/install/uninstall/serve, these messages dangle users with a non-existent flag suggestion. The task brief specifically asks whether to customize.
options: Rewrite to 'Stop the other serve process (PID ${state.pid}) first, then re-run this command.' (no flag suggestion, declarative remedy)
Rewrite to 'A `fab serve` instance (PID ${state.pid}) is holding the workspace lock. Stop it (Ctrl-C in that terminal or `kill ${state.pid}`) before running this command.' (verbose, max guidance)
Keep existing message structure; just delete the '--force to override' suffix. Minimal-diff.
Add a follow-up doctor lint that surfaces stale-lock detection for users who can't find the holding PID. Defer message change.

clarification_needs.question: Scenario 7 of install-diff-mode.test.ts (events.jsonl as directory + --force recovery): drop or migrate?
clarification_needs.context: packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-diff-mode.test.ts:298-L350+ tests that --force can recover from an events.jsonl-as-directory corruption (rc.14 TASK-004 Finding 2). Without --force, the install simply aborts with the canonical 'directory where file belongs' error. Three migration paths: (a) delete the scenario, accept that users must `fab uninstall && fab install` to recover, (b) port the directory→file recovery into the default install path (no --force needed), making this corruption always-auto-recoverable, (c) port to a new `fab doctor --fix` lint that detects + repairs the directory-where-file-belongs case.
options: Delete the scenario; document `fab uninstall && fab install` as the recovery path in error message
Move directory→file recovery into default install path (no flag needed) and rewrite the test against the new default behavior
Add a `fab doctor --fix` lint for events.jsonl-as-directory and move the test under doctor.test.ts
Keep --force alive ONLY for this corruption recovery (carve-out from the kill); rename to --recover-corrupt-state

clarification_needs.question: fab doctor --rescan: re-run runInitScan THEN doctor checks, or replace doctor checks?
clarification_needs.context: Proposed new flag from the task brief. runInitScan is already exported from scan.ts:178 and writes deterministic scan artifacts (.fabric/forensic.json, agents.meta.json refresh). User intent: get a fresh scan baseline before running doctor lints. Three semantics: (a) `fab doctor --rescan` runs runInitScan FIRST then continues with normal doctor lint/report (most useful — combined), (b) `fab doctor --rescan` REPLACES the doctor lint phase with scan output (scan-only mode), (c) `fab doctor --rescan` only fires runInitScan if doctor detects a 'scan stale' lint and bails out early otherwise.
options: Run runInitScan first, then continue with normal doctor checks (rescan + report in one invocation)
Replace doctor lint phase with scan output only (--rescan is a doctor-flavored alias for scan)
Conditional: only run runInitScan if doctor detects a 'scan stale' lint (current scan output > N days old)
Make --rescan a top-level command instead: `fab rescan` as a public sibling to install/uninstall/doctor/serve

clarification_needs.question: installHooks function preservation pattern: keep as library export, move, or inline?
clarification_needs.context: packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts hosts BOTH the citty `hooksCommand` (publicly deletable per C8 — it's already not in commands/index.ts's allCommands map) AND the `installHooks(target)` library function consumed by hooks-install-validate.test.ts and install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts. Deletion options: (a) delete the citty wrapper, keep installHooks as a named export from hooks.ts (file stays but stripped down), (b) move installHooks into packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts where its imports already originate; delete hooks.ts entirely, (c) inline installHooks's body at each test call site; delete hooks.ts; pay test duplication cost.
options: Delete the citty wrapper only; keep hooks.ts with the installHooks export (minimal-diff)
Move installHooks into packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts; delete hooks.ts entirely (cleaner module shape)
Inline installHooks at each call site; delete hooks.ts (eliminates the export but duplicates assembly)
Convert installHooks to a private helper, expose only its return value shape via a single thin wrapper in install/skills-and-hooks.ts

clarification_needs.question: --reapply flag: kill or preserve as the post-rc.15 'fix drift' affordance?
clarification_needs.context: The task brief explicitly lists --plan rename and --apply-lint rename, but does NOT explicitly list --reapply in C1-C9. After --force is killed, --reapply becomes the SOLE remaining install-time affordance for re-running over an existing canonical workspace. install.ts:619 ('legacy escape hatch deprecation warning'), install.ts:543 (`force: reapply ? true : args.force` — implies reapply currently forces internally), and install-cli-surface.test.ts:54 (`{force: true, reapply: true}`) all entangle force+reapply. Need explicit confirm: does rc.15 also kill --reapply?
options: Kill --reapply too; document that users always `fab uninstall && fab install` for a clean reset (matches the rc.14 deprecation guidance)
Keep --reapply as the only re-application path; decouple from --force semantics (--reapply re-writes managed files but respects drift-abort)
Keep --reapply AND make it force-overwrite by default (was the internal `force: reapply ? true` behavior at install.ts:543) — effectively renames --force to --reapply
Defer --reapply decision to rc.16; rc.15 only addresses C1-C10 as listed

_metadata.task_description: rc.15 Phase 2 schema-and-test-impact audit: (1) C10 dedupe `auditMode` / `audit_mode` in fabric-config schema + map all consumers (clean-slate, no shim); (2) comprehensive test surface impact map across all rc.15 cleanups (flag kills --force, command deletions hooks/scan, renames --plan→--dry-run + --apply-lint→--fix-knowledge, +--rescan addition, +C10 schema dedupe). Identify breaking tests, snapshot tests, and lock-conflict UX message audit.
